codeReview.summary: The feature adds a password-change endpoint and selective session-deletion query, but two blocking gaps remain: revocation failures are silently ignored (allowing old sessions to survive), and required security behaviors were not tested.

issues.description: If session revocation fails (`UserSessionDeleteOthersByTokenHash` or fallback path), the handler only logs a warning and still returns 200 OK. This violates the requirement that other sessions must be revoked immediately after password change; compromised sessions can remain valid with no client-visible failure.

The added tests cover helper parsing and input/auth validation, but do not verify the required session-invalidation behaviors (other sessions revoked, current session preserved, revoked sessions return 401 immediately, other users unaffected). The feature description explicitly requires these security tests, so core behavior is currently unproven.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `security-backend-worker` skill requires RED-first testing and coverage of the security behavior, but this worker explicitly skipped DB-backed assertions and still reported procedure compliance. The skill should include a concrete escalation path and/or standard approach when required assertions depend on DB/session state.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript skeleton: "No test DB infrastructure... I should follow this pattern and test what I can without DB" and "The actual end-to-end DB behavior will be verified at deployment time." Handoff `tests.coverage` also states DB-dependent behaviors were not directly tested while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There appears to be no documented, shared pattern for DB-backed handler tests in `internal/rpc`, which led to test-scope reduction on a security-critical feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows multiple searches for `TestMain|testDB|testSetup` in `internal/rpc` and `internal/database` returning no matches, followed by downgrading tests to helper-level only. `.factory/library/architecture.md` and mission docs do not provide a DB-test harness strategy for this case.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 499910e1517cfe269c2ca8e856cdac4d1b27d98b. Implementation is close, but fails review because session-revocation errors are non-fatal and required session-invalidation security behaviors were not actually tested.
